H1.name: Business & Operating Model
H1.description: Characteristics of the business itself that create inherent D&O exposure surfaces

H2.name: People & Management
H2.description: Characteristics of management and board that create exposure through inexperience or structural dependencies

H3.name: Financial Structure
H3.description: Financial structure characteristics that create inherent D&O claim exposure surfaces

H4.name: Governance Structure
H4.description: Structural governance characteristics that increase exposure by reducing accountability

H5.name: Public Company Maturity
H5.description: Where the company sits in its public company lifecycle

H6.name: External Environment
H6.description: External conditions that amplify or suppress D&O exposure

H7.name: Emerging / Modern Hazards
H7.description: New-era exposure sources not fully addressed in traditional frameworks

H1-01.name: Industry Sector Risk Tier
H1-01.description: Inherent litigation rate associated with the company's primary industry

H1-02.name: Business Model Complexity
H1-02.description: How difficult the business is to understand, explain, and evaluate from outside

H1-03.name: Regulatory Intensity
H1-03.description: Number and power of regulatory bodies overseeing operations

H1-04.name: Geographic Complexity
H1-04.description: Number and risk level of jurisdictions where the company operates

H1-05.name: Revenue Model Manipulation Surface
H1-05.description: How susceptible revenue recognition is to manipulation or aggressive accounting

H1-06.name: Customer & Supplier Concentration
H1-06.description: Revenue and supply chain dependency on a small number of counterparties

H1-07.name: Capital Intensity
H1-07.description: Degree of large, lumpy capital investments with long payback periods

H1-08.name: M&A Activity
H1-08.description: Level and nature of acquisition activity and integration complexity

H1-09.name: Speed of Growth
H1-09.description: Rate of revenue, headcount, and operational footprint growth

H1-10.name: Dual-Class Structure
H1-10.description: Multi-class share structure separating economic ownership from voting control

H1-11.name: Non-GAAP Reliance
H1-11.description: Extent of emphasis on non-GAAP financial measures in investor communications

H1-12.name: Technology Platform Dependency
H1-12.description: Dependency on third-party technology platforms for distribution or core operations

H1-13.name: IP Dependency
H1-13.description: Degree to which company value depends on IP that could be challenged or expired

H2-01.name: Management Public Company Experience
H2-01.description: Depth of CEO, CFO, and C-suite experience in running public companies

H2-02.name: Industry Expertise Match
H2-02.description: Whether CEO and CFO have deep experience in the company's specific industry

H2-03.name: Scale Experience Mismatch
H2-03.description: Whether management has experience operating at the current company's scale

H2-04.name: Board Governance Experience & Quality
H2-04.description: Collective experience, expertise diversity, and oversight capability of the board

H2-05.name: Founder-Led vs Professional Management
H2-05.description: Whether the company is led by its founder(s) or professional management

H2-06.name: Key Person Dependency
H2-06.description: Degree to which company value depends on specific individuals

H2-07.name: Management Turnover
H2-07.description: Recent C-suite turnover and reasons for departures

H2-08.name: Tone at the Top
H2-08.description: Observable indicators of corporate culture and management transparency attitude

H3-01.name: Leverage & Capital Structure
H3-01.description: Level of debt relative to equity and complexity of debt structure

H3-02.name: Off-Balance Sheet Arrangements
H3-02.description: Extent of VIEs, unconsolidated JVs, and special-purpose entities

H3-03.name: Goodwill-Heavy Balance Sheet
H3-03.description: Proportion of total assets represented by goodwill and acquired intangibles

H3-04.name: Earnings Quality Characteristics
H3-04.description: Structural susceptibility of earnings to manipulation independent of actual manipulation

H3-05.name: Cash Flow vs Earnings Divergence
H3-05.description: Structural tendency for cash flows to diverge from reported earnings

H3-06.name: Pre-Revenue / Cash Burn
H3-06.description: Whether the company is pre-revenue or early-revenue with significant cash burn

H3-07.name: Related Party Transactions
H3-07.description: Volume and nature of transactions between company and its officers/directors

H3-08.name: Capital Markets Activity
H3-08.description: Recent or pending securities offerings and capital raises

H4-01.name: Combined CEO / Board Chair
H4-01.description: Whether the CEO also serves as Chairman of the Board

H4-02.name: Board Independence
H4-02.description: Percentage of independent directors on the board and key committees

H4-03.name: Anti-Takeover Provisions
H4-03.description: Accumulation of anti-takeover defenses entrenching management

H4-04.name: Audit Committee Quality
H4-04.description: Expertise, activity level, and effectiveness of the audit committee

H4-05.name: Shareholder Rights
H4-05.description: Practical mechanisms for shareholders to hold management accountable

H4-06.name: Compensation Structure
H4-06.description: Whether compensation structure creates incentives that increase D&O risk

H4-07.name: Compliance Infrastructure
H4-07.description: Visible presence and quality of compliance and reporting infrastructure

H4-08.name: State of Incorporation
H4-08.description: Legal framework governing the company based on state of incorporation

H5-01.name: IPO Recency
H5-01.description: How recently the company completed its IPO (most powerful hazard dimension)

H5-02.name: Method of Going Public
H5-02.description: How the company became publicly traded (IPO, SPAC, direct listing, etc.)

H5-03.name: Exchange & Index Membership
H5-03.description: Which exchange and major indices the company belongs to

H5-04.name: FPI / ADR Status
H5-04.description: Whether the company is a foreign private issuer listed via ADR

H5-05.name: Seasoning & Track Record
H5-05.description: Track record of navigating public company reporting and market corrections

H6-01.name: Market Cycle Position
H6-01.description: Where the broader equity market sits in its cycle

H6-02.name: Industry Regulatory Spotlight
H6-02.description: Whether the company's industry is under heightened regulatory scrutiny

H6-03.name: Industry Litigation Wave
H6-03.description: Whether the industry is experiencing a wave of D&O-related litigation

H6-04.name: Political & Policy Environment
H6-04.description: Current political environment impact on regulatory enforcement

H6-05.name: Interest Rate Environment
H6-05.description: Prevailing interest rate environment and impact on leverage-sensitive companies

H6-06.name: Plaintiff Attorney Activity
H6-06.description: Current level of plaintiff attorney activity and resources in securities litigation

H6-07.name: Geopolitical Risk
H6-07.description: Geopolitical tensions, sanctions, and cross-border risks affecting operations

H7-01.name: AI Adoption & Governance
H7-01.description: Degree of AI use/development and maturity of AI governance framework

H7-02.name: Cybersecurity Governance
H7-02.description: Cyber risk profile and board-level cyber governance maturity

H7-03.name: ESG / Climate Exposure
H7-03.description: Exposure to ESG-related litigation and climate disclosure obligations

H7-04.name: Crypto / Digital Asset Exposure
H7-04.description: Involvement with cryptocurrency, blockchain, or digital asset operations

H7-05.name: Social Media / Public Persona Risk
H7-05.description: Risk from executive social media presence and public statements

H7-06.name: Workforce & Labor Model
H7-06.description: Workforce model risks including contractor classification and labor practices

missing_data_handling.low_coverage_note: IES based on limited data coverage
